data.name: Language_Subtitle
data.value: Select the language for the editor. You can change this any time in Settings.

data.name: Settings_Language_Hint
data.value: The editor's display language. Applies immediately.

data.name: Header_HomeTooltip
data.value: Home: start page with all detected worlds

data.name: Empty_EveryWriteKeepsABakBackupNextToTheSave
data.value: Every write keeps a .bak backup next to the save.

data.name: Empty_OpenYourSaveFolderInstructions
data.value: Open your save folder (usually …\AppData\Local\AbioticFactor\Saved\SaveGames\<steamid>\Worlds\<WorldName>), then pick a player or world save from the sidebar.

data.name: Empty_PlayersWorldsSummary
data.value: Players: stats · inventory · skills · traits · recipes · codex · achievements Worlds: containers · quest flags · doors · ground items · story progression

data.name: Empty_SaveFoldersDetectedAutomatically
data.value: Save folders detected automatically from the game client and any installed dedicated server. Pick one, or use OPEN FOLDER / drag a folder in.

data.name: Empty_YouCanAlsoDropASaveFolderAnywhereOnThisWindow
data.value: You can also drop a save folder anywhere on this window.

data.name: FileSidebar_AddAPlayerNewOrCopiedFromAnExistingOne
data.value: Add a player (new or copied from an existing one)

data.name: FileSidebar_ClickOpenFolderToPointAtACascadeSaveDirectory
data.value: Click OPEN FOLDER to point at a Cascade save directory.

data.name: Main_ParsingSaveLargeWorldSavesCanTakeAFewSeconds
data.value: PARSING SAVE: large world saves can take a few seconds…

data.name: Main_ShowOrHideTheSaveFileList
data.value: Show or hide the save-file list

data.name: Main_ShowOrHideTheSlotEditorAndItemCatalog
data.value: Show or hide the slot editor & item catalog

data.name: PlayerAchievements_CheckSteamTooltip
data.value: Verifies against steamcommunity.com (needs a public profile or an in-app sign-in) - adds icons, unlock dates and the authoritative unlock state. The local cache only refreshes while Steam runs.

data.name: PlayerAchievements_GatedProfileHelp
data.value: The editor checks Steam anonymously, so profiles with non-public Game details come back empty. Sign in to Steam inside the app and CHECK STEAM reuses that session (works for your own account and friends). Alternatively view the page in your browser, or make Game details public so the anonymous check succeeds.

data.name: PlayerAchievements_NoAchievementData
data.value: No achievement data - try CHECK STEAM.

data.name: PlayerAchievements_PrivacySettingsTooltip
data.value: Opens your Steam privacy settings - set 'Game details' to Public, wait a few minutes, then CHECK STEAM again.

data.name: PlayerAchievements_ShowSpoilersTooltip
data.value: Many of the game's achievements are hidden story spoilers. This mirrors the app-wide Spoiler protection setting (checked = show everything). You can also tap any single CLASSIFIED row to reveal just that one.

data.name: PlayerAchievements_SignInInAppTooltip
data.value: Opens Steam's sign-in page in a window inside the editor; the captured session is then used for the achievements check. Your password goes to Steam only.

data.name: PlayerAchievements_SignOutTooltip
data.value: Clears the stored Steam session cookie from memory and secure storage.

data.name: PlayerAchievements_SignedInTooltip
data.value: A Steam session cookie is stored (OS-protected) - CHECK STEAM queries as that account.

data.name: PlayerAchievements_ViewInBrowserTooltip
data.value: Opens the Steam community sign-in in your browser, then redirects to this profile's Abiotic Factor achievements page.

data.name: PlayerCharacter_BackgroundHelp
data.value: Changing the background does not re-apply its skill bonuses or starting gear; it only changes the save's job id.

data.name: PlayerCharacter_NoTraitsMatch
data.value: No traits match (or all are already applied).

data.name: PlayerCodex_FooterHelp
data.value: Emails, journal, compendium and fish states are all editable (stage + SAVE). Compendium entries unlock into the matching section lists automatically.

data.name: PlayerCodex_NothingMatches
data.value: Nothing matches (or game data is unavailable).

data.name: PlayerCodex_SearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search titles, senders and full text…

data.name: PlayerCodex_SelectAnEntry
data.value: Select an entry to read it.

data.name: PlayerGeneral_ChangeSteamIdHelp
data.value: Renames the file AND rewrites the save's internal SaveIdentifier (a .bak of the original is kept). Bed claims in the world still reference the old id.

data.name: PlayerGeneral_DiscoverAllItemsTooltip
data.value: Adds every catalog item to this player's seen-items list. Effect: no more yellow NEW badges in the inventory, and pickup-gated compendium hints treat everything as encountered. No quest or stat impact.

data.name: PlayerGeneral_DiscoveryHelp
data.value: What these do: ITEMS SEEN suppresses NEW badges; ITEMS CRAFTED marks first-craft history (used by craft-gated hints); MAPS reveals the sector map pamphlets in the GATEPal map screen. None of them advance quests, open doors or change stats.

data.name: PlayerGeneral_MapsTooltip
data.value: Maps are the paper pamphlets that reveal each sector's layout in the GATEPal map screen. UNLOCK ALL grants all of them as if picked up. Purely informational - it does not open doors or advance quests.

data.name: PlayerGeneral_RecipesHelp
data.value: Browse and toggle individual recipes in the RECIPES tab above.

data.name: PlayerInventory_DropItemTooltip
data.value: Select an occupied slot, then DROP places that item on the world ground near you. On SAVE it is written to the world save and appears in NEARBY GROUND ITEMS (.bak kept).

data.name: PlayerInventory_GroundNearbyHelp
data.value: Items dropped within reach of where this player last stood. PICK UP moves one into the first empty backpack slot (staged); SAVE writes the inventory and removes it from the ground (.bak kept). DISCARD leaves it lying there.

data.name: PlayerInventory_SortTooltip
data.value: Sorts the backpack: items grouped by category and name, empties last.

data.name: PlayerPets_CarryOverHelp
data.value: Health, level and name carry over. Save other player edits first.

data.name: PlayerPets_IntroHelp
data.value: Pets this player carries as items - in the Companion slot, the hotbar, or the backpack. Click one to rename it, change its variant, set its level (XP) or health, or remove it. Edits stage until SAVE. Move pets between a player and the world with the CLI ('pet send' / 'pet grab').

data.name: PlayerPets_NoBedHelp
data.value: This world has no pet bed - the pet will be placed at the world origin.

data.name: PlayerPets_NoPets
data.value: This player is carrying no pets.

data.name: PlayerPets_SelectAPet
data.value: Select a pet to edit it.

data.name: PlayerRaw_RawSaveJsonHelp
data.value: Save JSON is far too large to edit comfortably in-app. EXPORT writes a .json file next to the save and opens it in your default editor; make your changes there, save, then IMPORT to convert it back into the .sav (a .bak backup of the save is kept).

data.name: PlayerRecipes_FooterHelp
data.value: Click a recipe for its full wiki-style detail. Toggling a checkbox stages the change; press SAVE in the STRUCTURED tab header to write it.

data.name: PlayerRecipes_NoRecipesMatch
data.value: No recipes match (or game data is unavailable).

data.name: PlayerRecipes_SearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search recipes by item name or id…

data.name: PlayerSkills_LevelRangeHint
data.value: LEVEL 0–20 · SET LEVEL SNAPS XP TO THRESHOLD

data.name: PlayerSkills_MilestoneHelp
data.value: Tap a milestone for its full detail in the slot panel. Milestone counts vary per skill, and locked perks stay sealed (hidden in-game) until you reach the level - or tap one to override clearance.

data.name: PlayerSkills_RawXpTooltip
data.value: Raw XP; level re-derives from it.

data.name: PlayerSkills_XpSliderTooltip
data.value: Drag to set raw XP - the level re-derives from it.

data.name: PlayerSpawn_PickRegionTitle
data.value: Pick the region (level) of the spawn point…

data.name: PlayerSpawn_PickTerminalTitle
data.value: Pick the punch-card terminal to respawn at…

data.name: PlayerSpawn_SpawnHelp
data.value: The spawn point is the last safe location + region above (stage + SAVE). SET SPAWN HERE copies a bed's position; pick the region the bed is in if it differs. Sleeping bags rest without moving the spawn.

data.name: PlayerTransmog_TransmogHelp
data.value: Cosmetic items shown over each armor slot (the Transmogrifier's purple slots). Only the item id matters; stats stay on the real gear. Tap a slot to edit; stage + SAVE.

data.name: PlayerTransmog_Visibility
data.value: VISIBILITY (the eye toggles; unchecked hides that armor piece's visuals entirely)

data.name: Slot_AdditivelySetsEveryMissingPrerequisiteFlagStoryOrderSafeThenThisFlagCanBeEnabled
data.value: Additively sets every missing prerequisite flag (story-order safe), then this flag can be enabled.

data.name: Slot_AddsEveryChapterTriggerFlagFromTheStartOfTheStoryThroughThisChapterTheDependencySafeWayToUnlockItAdditiveOnlyNeverRemovesFlags
data.value: Adds every chapter trigger flag from the start of the story through this chapter - the dependency-safe way to unlock it. Additive only; never removes flags.

data.name: Slot_ChangesStageLikeEverythingElsePressSaveToWrite
data.value: Changes stage like everything else; press SAVE to write.

data.name: Slot_ChaptersAreStrictlyLinearAQuestCantReallyBeginUntilEveryEarlierTriggerFlagIsSetStageSaveToWrite
data.value: Chapters are strictly linear: a quest can't really begin until every earlier trigger flag is set. Stage + SAVE to write.

data.name: Slot_EmptySlotDragAnotherSlotsContentsHereToFillOrUseRawJsonToAddANewItem
data.value: EMPTY SLOT · Drag another slot's contents here to fill, or use RAW JSON to add a new item.

data.name: Slot_EnablingIsBlockedUntilPrerequisitesAreSetSinceTheGameNeverProducesOutOfOrderStoryStateStageSaveToWrite
data.value: Enabling is blocked until prerequisites are set, since the game never produces out-of-order story state. Stage + SAVE to write.

data.name: Slot_LiquidTypeWhatThisItemIsAllowedToHold
data.value: Liquid type (what this item is allowed to hold)…

data.name: Slot_OnlyItemsTheSelectedEquipmentSlotAcceptsAreListedSameRuleTheGameEnforcesUntickToBrowseEverything
data.value: Only items the selected equipment slot accepts are listed (same rule the game enforces). Untick to browse everything.

data.name: Slot_OpensACommunityInteractiveMapOfTheFacility
data.value: Opens a community interactive map of the facility

data.name: Slot_PerksAreDerivedFromTheSkillLevelTheGameGrantsThemAutomaticallyWhenYouReachTheLevelSoASaveEditorNeverWritesThemDirectly
data.value: Perks are derived from the skill level - the game grants them automatically when you reach the level, so a save editor never writes them directly.

data.name: Slot_ReadingTheLevelFromTheGameFiles
data.value: Reading the level from the game files...

data.name: Slot_ReplaceThisItemWithItsCraftingRecipeIngredientsSpreadIntoEmptySlotsLikeTheGamesSalvageStation
data.value: Replace this item with its crafting-recipe ingredients (spread into empty slots), like the game's salvage station.

data.name: Slot_SearchItemsByNameIdOrTag
data.value: Search items by name, id or tag…

data.name: Slot_SectorCardArtTheWikiHasNoPictureOfThisDoorType
data.value: Sector card art - the wiki has no picture of this door type.

data.name: Slot_SwapToTheUpgradedItemAtFullDurabilityTheEditorEquivalentOfAnUpgradeBenchUpgradeDTItemUpgrades
data.value: Swap to the upgraded item at full durability - the editor equivalent of an Upgrade Bench upgrade (DT_ItemUpgrades).

data.name: Slot_TapAnItemForItsEncyclopediaEntryDragOntoAnySlotToSpawnItFullStackDurabilityDoubleTapToGiveToTheFirstEmptySlot
data.value: Tap an item for its encyclopedia entry. Drag onto any slot to spawn it (full stack + durability); double-tap to give to the first empty slot.

data.name: Slot_TapAnyInventorySlotToEditItHere
data.value: Tap any inventory slot to edit it here.

data.name: Slot_TheLinkIsTheBenchsWorldGuidStoredOnTheItemSameAsSyncingAtABenchInGameStageSaveToWrite
data.value: The link is the bench's world GUID stored on the item - same as syncing at a bench in-game. Stage + SAVE to write.

data.name: Slot_TipDragASlotOntoAnotherToSwapDropOnAContainerInTheWorldEditorsListToMoveItemsBetweenContainers
data.value: Tip: drag a slot onto another to swap. Drop on a container in the world editor's list to move items between containers.

data.name: Slot_TradingIsBarterHandOverTheItemsAboveToBuyFromTheStockBelow
data.value: Trading is barter; hand over the items above to buy from the stock below.

data.name: Slot_UnlockingWritesTheChosenItemsFlagsPlusAnyFlagsNeededForTheTraderToAppearAtAllIntoWorldSaveFacilitySavABakIsKept
data.value: Unlocking writes the chosen items' flags (plus any flags needed for the trader to appear at all) into WorldSave_Facility.sav (a .bak is kept).

data.name: Slot_WhatTheySellTapAnItemToInspectTickLockedItemsToUnlock
data.value: WHAT THEY SELL · TAP AN ITEM TO INSPECT · TICK LOCKED ITEMS TO UNLOCK

data.name: Status_DiagnosticLogTooltip
data.value: Diagnostic log of save loads/writes, JSON export/import and asset mounting. Toggle in SETTINGS.

data.name: Status_DiffTwoSaveFilesOrTwoFoldersOfSavesWorldVsBackup
data.value: Diff two save files, or two folders of saves (world vs backup)

data.name: Status_ThemeLightDarkModeDiagnosticLoggingUsmapImport
data.value: Theme, light/dark mode, diagnostic logging, usmap import

data.name: WorldBases_BaseManagerIntro
data.value: Crafting benches anchor a base; everything deployed within ~30 m joins it. Select a base to see its composition and find it on the map (benches orange, containers green). Click one of its containers below and it opens right here over the map for editing.

data.name: WorldBases_CloseContainerTooltip
data.value: Close the container and show the base map again

data.name: WorldBases_ContainersInThisBaseClickToOpen
data.value: CONTAINERS IN THIS BASE: CLICK ONE TO OPEN IT ON THE MAP

data.name: WorldBases_NoContainersWithItemsInThisBase
data.value: No containers with items in this base.

data.name: WorldBases_NoCraftingBenchInThisGroup
data.value: No crafting bench in this group.

data.name: WorldBases_SlotEditorHint
data.value: Tap a slot to edit it in the slot editor; drag the catalog onto slots to add items.

data.name: WorldContainers_DragHelp
data.value: Drag a slot onto a container in the list to move the item into that container's first empty slot.

data.name: WorldContainers_FilterPlaceholder
data.value: Filter by class name or id…

data.name: WorldContainers_SelectContainerHelp
data.value: Pick a container from the list to inspect or edit its slots. Tap a slot to edit it in the right panel.

data.name: WorldContainment_ContainmentUnitsIntro
data.value: Entities currently trapped in deployed containment units (the metadata save's LeyakContainmentIDs map). Tap an entry to see what the creature looks like and which facility sector its unit sits in. RELEASE removes the link, so the creature roams free again on next load (the unit itself stays deployed in the Facility save). Staged until SAVE.

data.name: WorldContainment_NothingContained
data.value: Nothing is contained in this world right now. Build a containment unit and trap a creature (Leyak, Krasue) in-game; the entry appears here.

data.name: WorldContainment_ReleaseTooltip
data.value: Removes this creature's containment link (it escapes). Staged until SAVE; REVERT restores it.

data.name: WorldContainment_WorldUnlocksIntro
data.value: The game shares discovery world-wide on top of each player's own lists: items seen, emails, journal pages and compendium sections unlocked by ANY player land here. Bulk unlocks are additive and staged until SAVE.

data.name: WorldDoors_FilterPlaceholder
data.value: Filter doors by name, map, kind, lock type, or state…

data.name: WorldDoors_KeepStateTooltip
data.value: When on, the game keeps this door in the state you set and will not restore it to its default (usually locked) state when the area reloads. Leave off for normal doors.

data.name: WorldDoors_OneWayHelp
data.value: One-way doors only: marks the door as already unlocked from its locked side (opened once). Leave off for normal doors.

data.name: WorldDoors_SectorCaption
data.value: All doors below are in this sector; toggle their open/locked state, and positions are listed per door.

data.name: WorldDoors_SecurityStateHelp
data.value: Controls whether this security door is saved open or closed.

data.name: WorldDoors_SimpleStateHelp
data.value: Locked = stays locked until unlocked in-game; Open / Closed = mechanically open or closed. This sets the door's saved state.

data.name: WorldDropped_FilterPlaceholder
data.value: Filter ground items by name or id…

data.name: WorldDropped_GroundItemsIntro
data.value: Long-lived worlds accumulate hundreds of ground items, which costs real in-game performance. Mark items for deletion here (nothing is removed until you press SAVE; a .bak backup is kept). Search to target specific litter, e.g. 'rotten'.

data.name: WorldEditor_DayDiscoveredHelp
data.value: The in-game day this region was first entered (drives the day counter shown on its map pamphlet).

data.name: WorldFeature_EditHint
data.value: Click an entry to edit its fields. Edits stage until SAVE; every write keeps a .bak.

data.name: WorldFeature_NoEntries
data.value: No entries match (or this map has none in this save).

data.name: WorldFeature_SelectEntry
data.value: Select an entry to edit it.

data.name: WorldFeature_StagedForRemoval
data.value: Staged for removal - will be deleted on SAVE (resets this actor to its default).

data.name: WorldFlags_FilterPlaceholder
data.value: Filter by area, friendly or raw name…

data.name: WorldFlags_FlagsIntro
data.value: These are the quest flags this world has actually reached, the one-way switches stored in THIS save. The full story state lives in WorldSave_Facility.sav; region files carry only the flags for their own area. Setting one here can skip forward (the game may pull in prerequisites that cascade to the metadata save and other worlds); clearing one rewinds that event.

data.name: WorldFlags_HowQuestsWorkBody
data.value: The world remembers events as named flags - one-way switches the game sets when something happens (a door opened, an NPC met, a sector completed). The main story is a linear chain: Office > Manufacturing West > Labs > Security > Hydroplant > Reactors > Residence; each chapter is triggered by one specific flag (marked STORY below, numbered in story order). TOGGLE sets or clears a flag in this save: setting a STORY flag makes the world believe that event happened (skipping forward), clearing one rewinds it - but items/doors already changed by the event stay changed. Ordinary flags only gate small things like tutorial popups and TV tips. The current chapter pointer itself lives in WorldSave_MetaData's STORY tab.

data.name: WorldFlags_NoFlags
data.value: This save has no quest flags yet (or none match the filter).

data.name: WorldNpcs_NpcsIntro
data.value: Live story-NPC / trader records in this save. REVIVE brings a removed actor back; the narrative state byte controls their story stage (the game's enum has no readable names - change it only if you know the target stage). Tamed pets now have their own PETS tab.

data.name: WorldNpcs_ReviveTooltip
data.value: Bring this NPC back (alive + default script phase). Most dead entries are story-scripted departures, so reviving may resurrect an actor the plot already moved elsewhere.

data.name: WorldNpcs_ScriptPhaseTooltip
data.value: Game-internal dialogue/script phase (0-5), not quest progress. Every living NPC sits at 3.

data.name: WorldNpcs_StoryNpcsAndTraders
data.value: STORY NPCS & TRADERS IN THIS WORLD

data.name: WorldNpcs_TradersMoved
data.value: Looking for traders? They moved to the metadata save's TRADERS tab, where you can unlock their stock world-wide.

data.name: WorldPets_ArmorSetSummonNotice
data.value: This is an armor-set summon. It is shown for reference but is not editable.

data.name: WorldPets_CompanionSlotHint
data.value: Companion slot (off = first free hotbar slot)

data.name: WorldPets_NoPetsInThisWorldSave
data.value: No pets in this world save.

data.name: WorldPets_PerLimbHealthHelp
data.value: Health is per-limb current HP. The blueprint maximum is not stored, so HEAL raises every used limb to the pet's strongest limb. A pet with all limbs at 0 is 'downed' (critically wounded).

data.name: WorldPets_PetListIntro
data.value: Click a pet to edit it: rename, heal / down / revive, set level (XP), or change its creature type (the game's mutation - e.g. a Pest into another Pest, Peccary, or Skink variant). Delete removes it from the world. Armor-set summons (Exor, Mystagogue) are shown but not editable. Edits stage until SAVE; every write keeps a .bak.

data.name: WorldPets_SelectAPetToEditIt
data.value: Select a pet to edit it.

data.name: WorldPets_SendToPlayerHelp
data.value: Health, level and name carry over; the pet leaves the world. Save other world edits first.

data.name: WorldRaw_RawJsonIntro
data.value: World saves serialize to very large JSON (the Facility save is ~100 MB), so editing that in-app is not practical. EXPORT writes a .json file next to the save and opens it in your default editor; IMPORT converts your edited file back into the .sav (a .bak backup is kept).

data.name: WorldStory_ChaptersHeader
data.value: CHAPTERS: SET REWINDS OR SKIPS THE STORY AND SYNCS ITS TRIGGER FLAGS

data.name: WorldStory_ContainmentMoved
data.value: Containment units (trapped Leyak/Krasue) and world-wide unlocks moved to the CONTAINMENT tab.

data.name: WorldStory_NoRecipes
data.value: No recipes match (or game data is unavailable).

data.name: WorldStory_StorySplitIntro
data.value: How story state is split: THIS file (metadata) holds the current-chapter pointer; what a chapter shows as DONE comes from TRIGGER FLAGS stored in WorldSave_Facility.sav. Pressing SET handles both at once: it moves the pointer, adds every trigger flag up to that chapter and clears the flags past it (the Facility file is written immediately with a .bak; the pointer itself stages until SAVE).

data.name: WorldStory_WhyMetadata
data.value: Why is this on the METADATA file? The game stores world-wide research here (GlobalRecipesUnlocked/Researched): recipes researched once at a bench become available to EVERY player in the world. Per-player recipe knowledge is separate: edit it on a player save's RECIPES tab. Unlocking here updates both the unlocked and researched lists.

data.name: WorldTraders_TradersIntro
data.value: Everyone who barters in the Facility, from the game's own DT_NPC_Traders table plus wiki lore. A trader (and pieces of its stock) only appears once the matching world flag is set; those flags live in WorldSave_Facility.sav. Tap a card to open it in the side panel, where you can see each item with its icon, inspect what it is, and pick exactly which locked items to unlock. Trading itself is barter; no money is involved.

data.name: WorldTraders_SnapshotBody
data.value: The game isn't loaded, so this shows the editor's built-in trader snapshot. The trades and the world flags that unlock them (for example Dr. Carson's gear) are accurate and editable here, but item names and icons need the installed game. To load it, use Settings > Game Data ("Locate game folder"); auto-detection only finds Steam installs, so Game Pass, Epic, or moved libraries need to be set manually.

data.name: WorldVehicles_DisplayOnlyNoEditableState
data.value: Display only - this vehicle has no editable drivable / wrecked / move state.

data.name: WorldVehicles_NoVehiclesInThisSave
data.value: No vehicles in this save (vehicles live in region saves).

data.name: WorldVehicles_SelectAVehicleToEditIt
data.value: Select a vehicle to edit it.

data.name: WorldVehicles_VehicleListIntro
data.value: Click a vehicle to edit it: toggle whether it is drivable (unlocked) or wrecked, move it, reset it to its original spawn position, or open its on-board storage. Edits stage until SAVE; every write keeps a .bak.

data.name: Compare_ModeCardDescription
data.value: Two player saves get a readable, side-by-side summary (recipes, fish, skills…); any saves also expose the full raw property diff. Folder mode diffs a world against one of its backups.

data.name: Compare_PickTwoMessage
data.value: Choose both A and B before comparing.

data.name: Compare_SavesTooLarge
data.value: Saves too large to flatten completely - some differences may be missing.

data.name: Compare_NoMeaningfulDiff
data.value: No meaningful differences in the tracked categories.

data.name: Compare_CopiedMessage
data.value: The comparison report was copied to the clipboard as Markdown.

data.name: Compare_ExportCardDescription
data.value: Copy the full comparison as a Markdown report, or save it to a .md file.

data.name: Compare_WhatsDifferentDescription
data.value: A quick map of the categories that changed - details follow below.

data.name: Compare_ShowNoiseToggle
data.value: Show {0} identity / clock / instance / position difference(s)

data.name: Compare_RawPropertyDiffDescription
data.value: Every property-level difference ({0} gameplay, {1} other). Deep-dive when the summary isn't enough.

data.name: Compare_FolderSummary
data.value: {0} differing · {1} identical · {2} only in A · {3} only in B

data.name: Compare_TapDifferingFile
data.value: Tap a differing file to see its property differences.

data.name: CreateWorld_PlatformCardDesc
data.value: Which copy of the game is this world for? You can also add a copy for the other platform on the final step.

data.name: CreateWorld_PlatformSteamDesc
data.value: Steam - a loose-file world folder (WorldSave_*.sav + PlayerData/Player_*.sav) under the Steam SaveGames tree. Players are owned by a 17-digit SteamID64.

data.name: CreateWorld_PlatformGamePassDesc
data.value: Game Pass / Microsoft Store - an Xbox "wgs" container (the same saves packed into one Oodle-compressed bundle). Players are owned by an Xbox account id (XUID).

data.name: CreateWorld_WorldCardDesc
data.value: Choose a name and the parent folder where the new world folder will be created.

data.name: CreateWorld_WorldNameRequiredMsg
data.value: Please enter a name for the new world folder.

data.name: CreateWorld_LocationNotFoundMsg
data.value: The save location folder does not exist. Please choose an existing folder.

data.name: CreateWorld_AlreadyExistsMsg
data.value: A non-empty folder named '{0}' already exists at that location. Choose a different name or location.

data.name: CreateWorld_PlayersCardDesc
data.value: Each player needs their own owner id. Pick a known account on this machine, or choose "Custom id..." to type a {0} in. You can add more players later via the + button in the sidebar.

data.name: CreateWorld_PlayerIdNeededMsg
data.value: Player {0}: pick an account or type a custom id before continuing.

data.name: CreateWorld_IdNotUsableMsg
data.value: Player {0}: "{1}" can't name a save file. Use letters, digits, '-', '_' or '.' only (a SteamID64 looks like 76561198000000000).

data.name: CreateWorld_DuplicateIdMsg
data.value: Each player must have a unique owner id.

data.name: CreateWorld_DifficultyCardDesc
data.value: Sets the GameDifficulty and related multipliers in SandboxSettings.ini. You can edit them in the Config tab after creation.

data.name: CreateWorld_DiffCasualDesc
data.value: Casual - reduced enemy difficulty, boosted XP and item stacks. Perfect for exploring.

data.name: CreateWorld_DiffNormalDesc
data.value: Normal - balanced challenge, the intended experience for most players.

data.name: CreateWorld_DiffSurvivalDesc
data.value: Survival - harder enemies, natural item stacks, higher spawn rate.

data.name: CreateWorld_DiffNightmareDesc
data.value: Nightmare - maximum difficulty: reduced XP, heavy enemy damage, relentless spawns.

data.name: CreateWorld_ReviewCardDesc
data.value: Check the details below, then press CREATE WORLD.

data.name: CreateWorld_AlsoWriteCopy
data.value: Also write a {0} copy next to it

data.name: CreateWorld_RegionNote
data.value: Only WorldSave_MetaData.sav exists yet - the game writes each region/facility save the first time you load the world and visit that area.

data.name: CreateWorld_StatusGamePassSkipped
data.value: Game Pass packing was skipped (see the log) - opened the Steam folder "{0}" instead. {1}

data.name: CreateWorld_StatusBothOpenedGamePass
data.value: Created the world for both platforms. Opened the Game Pass container. {0}

data.name: CreateWorld_StatusBothCreated
data.value: Created the world for both platforms (Steam folder + Game Pass container next to it). {0}

data.name: Diff_OnlyInAB
data.value: {0} only in A · {1} only in B

data.name: Main_AddPlayerNeedFolder
data.value: Open a world's save folder first so the editor knows where to create the new player file.

data.name: Main_AddPlayerCopyPrompt
data.value:
Build a new player save in this world.
Start fresh, or copy “{0}” (keeps their inventory, skills and progress)?

data.name: Main_AddPlayerBlankTip
data.value:
Build a new blank player save in this world.
Tip: to copy an existing player instead, select them in the list first, then press +.

data.name: Main_AddPlayerBlank
data.value: Build a new blank player save in this world.

data.name: Main_PlayerCopied
data.value: Copied player to {0} - set their SteamID to {1}.

data.name: Main_NewPlayerIdMessage
data.value: Enter the new player's owner id. Usually a 17-digit SteamID64 (for example 76561198000000000); for Game Pass / Epic saves enter the account folder id.

data.name: Main_InvalidPlayerIdMessage
data.value: That isn't a valid id. Use letters, digits, '-', '_' or '.' (a Steam id looks like 76561198000000000).

data.name: Main_PlayerAlreadyExists
data.value: Player_{0}.sav already exists in this world. Choose a different id.

data.name: Main_LogFolderOpenFailed
data.value: Could not open the log folder: {0}

data.name: Main_PickMappingsTitle
data.value: Select a Mappings.usmap dumped from the game

data.name: Main_MappingsInstalledStatus
data.value: New mappings installed - press RELOAD GAME DATA (or restart) to load them.

data.name: Main_MappingsInstalledMessage
Main_DeviceSearching
data.value: Searching the world's region saves for the plugged-in device...

data.name: Main_DeviceNotFound
data.value: Could not find the plugged-in device in any region save here - it may have been removed.

data.name: Main_DeviceNoInventory
data.value: The plugged-in device is a {0}{1} - it has no inventory to open.

data.name: Main_DeviceInOtherFolder
data.value: The device is a {0} in {1}, which isn't in the loaded folder.

data.name: Main_DeviceNavFailed
data.value: Could not navigate to the device: {0}

data.name: Main_UnsavedChangesMessage
data.value: You have staged edits that haven't been written to the save yet. Save them, discard them, or stay on this editor.

data.name: Main_BedOtherPlayerMessage
data.value:
This bed is claimed by {0}. Setting your spawn here reassigns the claim to this player and leaves {0} with NO claimed bed (they respawn at the fallback spawn).
The world save is rewritten immediately (.bak kept). Continue?

data.name: Main_BedReassigned
data.value: Bed reassigned to {0} and spawn moved there (world save rewritten, .bak kept). Press SAVE to write the player's spawn point.

data.name: Main_BedClaimed
data.value: Bed claimed for {0} and spawn moved there (world save rewritten, .bak kept). Press SAVE to write the player's spawn point.

data.name: Main_BedClaimNotRewritten
data.value: Spawn moved, but the bed claim could not be rewritten (deployable not found in the Facility save).

data.name: Main_SpawnMoved
data.value: Spawn moved to {0} - press SAVE to write it. Check the level picker matches the bed's region.

data.name: Main_NoEmptyHotbarSlot
data.value: No empty hotbar slot - pets can only go in the hotbar or Companion slot.

data.name: Main_NoEmptyBackpackSlot
data.value: No empty backpack slot - drop or move something first.

data.name: Main_PickupStaged
data.value: {0} staged into slot {1}. SAVE writes the inventory and removes it from the ground (.bak kept).

data.name: Main_BedLookupUnavailable
data.value: No world save found next to this file - bed lookup unavailable.

data.name: Main_NoClaimedBed
data.value: No claimed bed found in this world (respawns at the fallback spawn).

data.name: Main_EditingIni
data.value: Editing {0} - every save keeps a .bak of the previous file.

data.name: Main_GpReadFailedTitle
data.value: Couldn't read the Game Pass save

data.name: Main_GpNoWorldsTitle
data.value: No worlds in this Game Pass save

data.name: Main_GpNoWorldsMessage
data.value: That folder is an Xbox container store but holds no editable world saves.

data.name: Main_GpOpened
data.value: Opened Game Pass world "{0}". SAVE writes straight back into the Xbox container (a backup is kept).

data.name: Main_GpOpenFailedTitle
data.value: Couldn't open the Game Pass save

data.name: Main_GpSaved
data.value: Saved into the Game Pass save for "{0}" (backup kept).

data.name: Main_GpWriteFailedTitle
data.value: Couldn't write the Game Pass save

data.name: Main_GpWriteFailedMessage
data.value: Your edit was saved to the working copy, but packing it back into the Xbox container failed: {0}

data.name: Main_SortedStacks
data.value: Sorted {0} stack(s) - press SAVE to write.

data.name: Main_DropSelectFirst
data.value: Select an occupied slot first, then DROP places it on the ground.

data.name: Main_Dropped
data.value: Dropped {0} on the ground near you (shown in NEARBY GROUND ITEMS). SAVE writes it into {1} (.bak kept); REVERT undoes it.

data.name: Main_DropRemovedNoTarget
data.value: Removed {0} (couldn't drop it into the world - this region has no existing ground item to clone the format from). Press SAVE to write.

data.name: Main_DismantleNoRecipe
data.value: {0} has no crafting recipe - nothing to dismantle it into.

data.name: Main_DismantleNeedsSlots
data.value: Dismantling needs {0} empty slot(s) - only {1} available.

data.name: Main_DismantleDestEquipment
data.value: All stacks go to empty backpack slots; this equipment slot is emptied.

data.name: Main_DismantleDestRegular
data.value: The first stack replaces this slot; the rest fill empty slots in the same inventory.

data.name: Main_TeleporterSynced
data.value: Teleporter synced to {0} - press SAVE to write it.

data.name: Main_BenchListUnavailable
data.value: No world save found next to this file - bench list unavailable.

data.name: Main_LoadingBenches
data.value: Loading world benches (parsing the Facility save)…

data.name: Main_SyncedToOtherRegion
data.value: Synced to: {0} (a bench in another region save).

data.name: Main_SyncedToMissing
data.value: Synced to bench {0}… (not found in the loaded saves - it may have been removed).

data.name: Main_GameDataLoaded
data.value: Game data loaded. Reopen your save to refresh item names, icons and traders.

data.name: Main_ChangeIdInvalid
data.value: Enter a valid id (a 17-digit SteamID64 like 76561198000000000, or any safe token for a non-Steam save).

data.name: Main_ChangeIdFileExists
data.value: Player_{0}.sav already exists in this folder.

data.name: Main_ChangeIdClaimsNotMigrated
data.value: Bed claims were NOT migrated (the old and new ids differ in length); any beds claimed by the old id stay under the old id.

data.name: Main_ChangeIdNoClaimsFound
data.value: No bed claims by the old id were found in the world saves.

data.name: Main_ChangeIdDoneWithClaims
data.value: Player save now belongs to {0}; {1} bed claim(s) in the world saves were updated too (.bak kept per file).

data.name: Main_ChangeIdDone
data.value: Player save now belongs to {0} (file renamed and SaveIdentifier rewritten).{1}

data.name: Main_ReloadMessage
data.value: Reloading re-reads the save from disk and discards the staged edits you haven't written yet. This cannot be undone.

data.name: GameDataPrompt_NoGameDataMessage
GameDataPrompt_PickUsmapTitle
data.value: Select a Mappings.usmap dumped from the game

data.name: PlayerEditor_OwnerIdNote
data.value: Game Pass / non-Steam account - this id is locked here. To change it, use SAVE CONVERSION in Settings (it can re-home the player while converting).

data.name: PlayerEditor_CutTraitDescription
data.value: (unused/cut trait - the game has no description or creator entry for it)

data.name: PlayerGeneral_ChangeSteamIdMessage
data.value:
This renames the save file to Player_{0}.sav so the game loads it for that account. A .bak copy of the original is kept.
World-side bed claims keyed by the old id are rewritten too, so the player keeps their claimed beds (each world save also keeps a .bak).

data.name: WorldNpcs_RevivedStatus
data.value: {0} revived - press SAVE to write. Story-scripted departures may reappear in-game.

data.name: WorldDropped_InspectingStatus
data.value: Inspecting dropped {0} - edit it in the sidebar (stage + SAVE).

data.name: Plugins_IntroHint
data.value: Plugins extend the editor with new save operations, commands, and panels. They run with full trust - only install plugins you trust. Drop plugin folders under: {0}

data.name: Plugins_NoOpenSaveHint
data.value: Open a save to run save operations against it.

data.name: Plugins_OpenSaveHint
data.value: Open save: {0} ({1}). Running an operation writes the file immediately (a .bak is kept) and reloads the editor, discarding any unsaved edits.

data.name: Plugins_NoOperationApplies
data.value: No installed operation applies to this save kind.

data.name: Plugins_ManifestUpdateFailed
data.value: Could not update the plugin manifest (read-only folder?).

data.name: Plugins_RunConfirmMessage
data.value:
Run '{0}' on {1}?
This writes the file immediately (a .bak is kept) and reloads the editor, discarding unsaved edits.

data.name: Plugins_WebToolFailed
data.value: The web tool failed to open: {0}

data.name: Plugins_ToolViewUnsupported
data.value: This tool returned a view the host could not display.

data.name: Settings_ThemeHint
data.value: Accent family for the editor chrome. Applies immediately; the editor view reloads.

data.name: Settings_DiagnosticsHint
data.value: Logs save loads/writes, staged edits, JSON transfers and unrecognized save content. Off by default.

data.name: Settings_NothingRevealed
data.value: Nothing has been individually revealed yet.

data.name: Settings_SpoilersHint
data.value: Seals content you haven't reached yet (future quest flags, traders, recipes, hidden achievements, codex entries, contained anomalies) behind a CLASSIFIED stamp. Tap any sealed item to override clearance and reveal it - revealed items stay visible from then on.

data.name: Settings_PluginsHint
data.value: Extend the editor with save operations, commands, panels, menu actions, and event hooks (managed or JavaScript). {0} plugin(s) installed. Toggle one off to stop loading it (restart to apply). Plugins run with full trust - only install plugins you trust.

data.name: Settings_PluginManifestFailed
data.value: Could not update the plugin manifest (read-only folder?).

data.name: Settings_ConvertIdPlaceholder
data.value: leave blank to keep existing ids

data.name: Settings_ConvertIdHint
data.value: Optional. A 17-digit SteamID64 (Steam) or account folder id (Game Pass). The player save is re-homed to it so the world is yours on the target platform. Single-player only.

data.name: Settings_ConvertToGamePassHint
data.value: Pick a Steam world folder (WorldSave_*.sav + PlayerData). Writes a Game Pass copy next to it.

data.name: Settings_ConvertToSteamHint
data.value: Pick a Game Pass folder (the one with containers.index). Writes a Steam world folder next to it.

data.name: Settings_Converting
data.value: Converting… (the first run may download the Oodle compressor)

data.name: Settings_ConvertDone
Settings_ConvertNoWorldSave
data.value: That folder has no WorldSave_*.sav files - pick a Steam world folder.

data.name: Settings_ConvertNoContainers
data.value: That folder has no containers.index - pick a Game Pass save folder.

data.name: Settings_SaveConversionHint
data.value: Convert a world between Steam (loose .sav files) and Game Pass / Xbox (one container). The save content is identical either way; only the packaging changes. The converted copy is written next to the folder you pick - your original is untouched.

data.name: Settings_OnLatestVersion
data.value: You are on the latest version.

data.name: Settings_UpdatesNotConfigured
data.value: Updates are not configured yet (no release repository set).

data.name: Settings_InstallUpdateMessage
data.value: Download and install {0}? The app will close and reopen.

data.name: Settings_InstallingRestart
data.value: Installing update; the app will restart...

data.name: Settings_UpdatesHintPlaceholder
data.value: Checks GitHub releases for a newer build. (No release repository is configured yet - the developer must set it before this works.)

data.name: Settings_UpdatesHint
data.value: Checks GitHub releases for a newer build and installs it in place. Every save still keeps its .bak.

data.name: GameDataSettings_LoadedAuto
data.value: Loaded from your auto-detected game install.

data.name: GameDataSettings_LoadedCustom
data.value: Loaded from the folder you set.

data.name: GameDataSettings_UsmapNotFound
data.value: (none found - the game's tables can't be read)

data.name: GameDataSettings_GameDataLoadedMessage
data.value: Game data loaded. Reopen your save to refresh traders and item icons.

data.name: GameDataSettings_NoGameDataMessage
GameDataSettings_CardHint
data.value: Traders, item icons and recipes come from your installed copy of the game, not from the save. Auto-detection finds Steam installs; for a Game Pass, Epic, or moved install, set your game folder here. If a game update stops the data from loading, import an updated data file (.usmap).

data.name: GameDataSettings_LoadModsHint
data.value: Mount mods from the game's ~mods and LogicMods folders so modded items show real names, icons and stats.

data.name: Slot_MsgNoBoundContext
data.value: Slot tap: no bound context found

data.name: Slot_MsgDraggingFromCatalog
data.value: Dragging {0} from catalog - drop on any slot…

data.name: Slot_MsgNoEmptyHotbar
data.value: No empty hotbar slot - pets can only go in the hotbar or Companion slot.

data.name: Slot_MsgNoEmptyAvailable
data.value: No empty slot available to give the item to.

data.name: Slot_MsgTickAtLeastOne
data.value: Tick at least one locked item to unlock.

data.name: Slot_FacilityWrittenNow
data.value: The Facility save is written immediately (a .bak is kept).

data.name: Slot_ChangesStaged
data.value: Changes are staged; press SAVE to write.

data.name: Slot_UnlockItemsBody
Slot_MsgTraderFullyUnlocked
data.value: {0} is already fully unlocked in this world.

data.name: Slot_UnlockAllBody
Slot_MsgChapterAlreadySet
data.value: All story flags through "{0}" are already set.

data.name: Slot_MsgChapterWroteFacility
data.value: Wrote {0} story flag(s) to WorldSave_Facility.sav (backup kept).

data.name: Slot_MsgChapterAdded
data.value: Added {0} story flag(s) through "{1}" - press SAVE to write.

data.name: Slot_SetPrereqsBody
Slot_MsgPrereqsSet
data.value: Set {0} prerequisite flag(s) - "{1}" can now be enabled. Press SAVE to write.

data.name: Spoiler_ClassifiedHint
data.value: Above your current clearance. Tap to override and reveal (you'll keep seeing it).

data.name: Spoiler_OverrideMessage
data.value:
{0} is sealed above your current clearance because it may spoil content you haven't reached yet.
Reveal it anyway? It will stay visible from now on.

data.name: Dialog_BulkConfirmMessage
data.value:
This will {0}.
The change stages until you press SAVE (REVERT undoes it before saving).

data.name: SteamLogin_PrivacyNote
data.value: Sign in on Steam's own page below - the editor never sees your password, only the resulting session cookie, which stays on this device (OS-protected storage) and is cleared by SIGN OUT.

data.name: SteamLogin_WindowsOnlyFallback
data.value: In-app Steam sign-in is currently Windows-only. Use your browser instead:

data.name: SteamLogin_SessionReadError
data.value: Could not read the sign-in session ({0}). Cancel and use VIEW IN BROWSER as a fallback.
